narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: White Garcetti was away, Los Angeles continued to struggle with the high crime and homelessness that have come to define his legacy, as well as a crisis in the City Council triggered by racist comments from City Council members within Garcetti’s party.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement criticizes Mayor Garcetti's competence and priorities by highlighting domestic crises (crime, homelessness, political scandal) that persisted while he was abroad, challenging his effectiveness and the integrity of his focus on international climate action over local governance.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: It is not clear why the conference could not be held remotely.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement implicitly criticizes the climate movement's practices by suggesting its key actors (mayors at a summit) are not employing readily available, low-carbon alternatives (remote attendance), thereby challenging the movement's operational integrity and commitment to its stated goals.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: White Garcetti was away, Los Angeles continued to struggle with the high crime and homelessness that have come to define his legacy, as well as a crisis in the City Council triggered by racist comments from City Council members within Garcetti’s party.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement criticizes Mayor Garcetti's competence and priorities by highlighting domestic crises (crime, homelessness, political scandal) that persisted while he was abroad, challenging his effectiveness and integrity as an authority figure.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: It is not clear why the conference could not be held remotely.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement implicitly criticizes the climate movement's actions by suggesting its key events (like the C40 summit) are unnecessarily held in-person, thereby questioning the movement's operational logic and commitment to its own principles.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: burning fossil fuels on an international flight rather than attending remotely and attending to pressing issues at home.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: CC: Criticism of institutions and authorities
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: though L.A.’s infrastructure is not widely considered “high-quality.” A proposed high-speed rail between L.A. and San Francisco has been largely canceled, and current passenger rail service from L.A. to San Diego has been suspended because of a potential landslide hazard.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote challenges the competence and credibility of the local government and its transportation initiatives, undermining the authority's claims about infrastructure quality and climate action.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Garcetti’s “Green New Deal” calls for shuttering natural gas plants; it has been opposed by public works unions, and lost the only serious electoral challenge it faced in a 2019 city council race.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The quote presents the climate policy (Green New Deal) as politically unpopular and opposed by key groups, challenging the movement's practical support and electoral viability.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: That is one-fourth (economy) or one-half (premium) the global average per capita use of carbon per year. It is not clear why the conference could not be held remotely.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: By quantifying the flight's carbon footprint relative to an annual average and questioning the necessity of in-person attendance, the text minimizes the significance of the individual action in the broader context of climate change, suggesting the concern is overblown.
